en.extract:
The white-throated rock thrush (Monticola gularis) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae of the order Passeriformes.
The bird's natural habitats include temperate forests.


Description, behavior, and diet
The white-throated rock thrush weighs 34 grams on average, although its weight ranges from 32 to 37 grams. The bird's length ranges from 16 to 19 centimetres (6.3 to 7.5 in). Its typical generation length is 3.8 years.
Adult male white-throated rock thrushes have white patches on their chins and throats. However, the rest of their undersides are a chestnut-orange color. Adult females have brown or olive-brown upper parts and "boldly scalloped" lower parts. Young males have gray or golden-brown feathers and orange undersides. The birds are also cobalt blue and black in places.  The bird's song has been described as "melancholy, flute-like, drawn-out rising whistles".


Behavior
White-throated rock thrushes inhabit their breeding grounds between May and September. Their breeding grounds are in mixed montane forests. They breed between May and July, forming two broods. The birds' cup-shaped nests consist of tree matter, lichen, rootlets, and moss on the inside and pine needles and stems on the outside.
The diet of white-throated rock thrushes consists mainly of invertebrates. These include weevils, mole crickets, and lepidopterans.
The white-throated rock thrush is a fully migrant species.


Distribution and habitat
The white-throated rock thrush has a very sizable range, 1,710,000 square kilometers. It is native and breeds in Manchuria, the Russian Far East and neighboring areas; it winters in Indochina and southern China. The birds are occasionally seen in Japan. They have also been observed in Hong Kong and Singapore. The bird's population is unknown, but is not increasing or declining.
The white-throated rock thrush is said to be rare in North Korea. However, it is relatively common in China, except on the coast.
The bird's IUCN Red List category is "least concern".
The white-throated rock thrush inhabits forested areas, shrubland and rocky areas. It lives in altitudes ranging between 0 metres (0 ft) and 1,500 metres (4,900 ft).


History
The white-throated rock thrush was first described by Robert Swinhoe in 1863.
Between 1988 and 2000, the bird's IUCN Red List categories have been "lower risk" and "least concern". Since 2000, its only category has been "least concern".
In modern times, white-throated rock thrushes are widely kept as pets. They are also consumed in some countries.


References


External links

White-throated rock thrush videos and photos on the Internet Bird Collection

The '''white-throated rock thrush''' ('''''Monticola gularis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Muscicapidae]] of the order [[Passeriformes]].<ref name="iucn status 11 November 2021" />

The bird's [[natural habitat]]s include [[temperate forest]]s.

==Description, behavior, and diet==
The white-throated rock thrush weighs 34 grams on average, although its weight ranges from 32 to 37 grams.<ref name = "moreinfo"/><ref name = "hbw">{{citation| last1=Collar | first1=Nigel | editor-first1=Josep | editor-first2=Andrew | editor-first3=Jordi | editor-first4=David | editor-first5=Eduardo | editor-last1=Del Hoyo | editor-last2=Elliott | editor-last3=Sargatal | editor-last4=Christie | editor-last5=De Juana |url = http://www.hbw.com/species/white-throated-rock-thrush-monticola-gularis|title = White-throated Rock-thrush (''Monticola gularis'')| chapter=White-throated Rock-Thrush (Monticola gularis) |year = 2014| doi=10.2173/bow.wtrthr1.01 |access-date = June 25, 2014}}</ref> The bird's length ranges from {{convert|16|to|19|cm|in}}.<ref name = "hbw"/> Its typical generation length is 3.8 years.<ref name = "moreinfo">{{Citation|url = http://www.birdlife.org/datazone/speciesfactsheet.php?id=6293&m=1|title = White-throated Rock-thrush ''Monticola gularis''|year = 2014|access-date = June 25, 2014}}</ref>

Adult male white-throated rock thrushes have white patches on their chins and throats.<ref name = "hbw"/><ref name = "thrushes">{{Citation|author = Peter Clement|author2 = Ren Hathway|url = https://books.google.com/books?id=IxBwBXK5HX0C&q=%22White-throated+rock+thrush%22&pg=PA199|title = Thrushes|date = 30 August 2010| publisher=A&C Black |isbn = 978-1-4081-3542-6|access-date = June 25, 2014}}</ref> However, the rest of their undersides are a chestnut-orange color.<ref name = "hbw"/> Adult females have brown or olive-brown upper parts and "boldly scalloped" lower parts. Young males have gray or golden-brown feathers and orange undersides.<ref name = "thrushes"/> The birds are also [[cobalt blue]] and black in places.<ref name = "birds of east asia"/>  The bird's [[birdsong|song]] has been described as "melancholy, [[flute]]-like, drawn-out rising whistles".<ref name = "hbw"/>

==Behavior==
White-throated rock thrushes inhabit their [[breeding ground]]s between May and September. Their breeding grounds are in mixed [[montane forest]]s. They breed between May and July, forming two broods. The birds' cup-shaped nests consist of tree matter, [[lichen]], rootlets, and [[moss]] on the inside and [[pine needle]]s and [[Plant stem|stems]] on the outside.<ref name = "hbw"/>

The diet of white-throated rock thrushes consists mainly of [[invertebrate]]s. These include [[weevil]]s, [[mole cricket]]s, and [[lepidopterans]].<ref name = "hbw"/>

The white-throated rock thrush is a fully migrant species.<ref name = "moreinfo"/>

==Distribution and habitat==
The white-throated rock thrush has a very sizable range, 1,710,000 square kilometers. It is native and breeds in [[Manchuria]], the [[Russian Far East]] and neighboring areas; it winters in [[Indochina]] and southern [[China]].<ref name = "hbw"/><ref name = "birdlife"/> The birds are occasionally seen in [[Japan]].<ref name = "birds of east asia">{{Citation|author = Mark Brazil|url = https://books.google.com/books?id=dkoroKb6yuIC&q=%22White-throated+rock+thrush%22&pg=PA430|title = Birds of East Asia|date = January 1, 2009| publisher=A&C Black |isbn = 978-1-4081-0874-1|access-date = June 25, 2014}}</ref> They have also been observed in [[Hong Kong]] and [[Singapore]].<ref name = "moreinfo"/> The bird's population is unknown, but is not increasing or declining.<ref name = "birdlife">{{Citation|url = http://www.birdlife.org/datazone/speciesfactsheet.php?id=6293|title = White-throated Rock-thrush Monticola gularis|year = 2014|access-date = June 25, 2014}}</ref>

The white-throated rock thrush is said to be rare in North Korea.<ref name = "birdlife"/> However, it is relatively common in China, except on the coast.<ref name = "hbw"/><ref name = "birds of east asia"/>

The bird's [[IUCN Red List]] category is "least concern".<ref name = "birdlife"/>

The white-throated rock thrush inhabits forested areas, [[shrubland]] and rocky areas. It lives in altitudes ranging between {{convert|0|m|ft}} and {{convert|1500|m|ft}}.<ref name = "moreinfo"/>

==History==
The white-throated rock thrush was first described by [[Robert Swinhoe]] in 1863.<ref name = "birdlife"/>

Between 1988 and 2000, the bird's IUCN Red List categories have been "lower risk" and "least concern". Since 2000, its only category has been "least concern".<ref name = "moreinfo"/>

In modern times, white-throated rock thrushes are widely kept as pets. They are also consumed in some countries.<ref name = "moreinfo"/>

zh.extract:
白喉磯鶇（學名：Monticola gularis）为鶲科磯鶇屬下的一種鳥類。這個物種是一種侯鳥，在俄羅斯的貝加爾湖以東地區、中國東北及朝鮮半島北部繁殖，在華南及東南亞過冬。雌雄體色有所差異，其中雄鳥以鈷藍色及栗紅色為主；雌鳥則以灰褐、白、黑為主。
這種鳥的自然棲地包括溫帶森林。


描述、行為與飲食
白喉磯鶇平均體重為34克，範圍在32至37克之間。 鳥的體長範圍從16至19厘米（6.3至7.5英寸） 其典型的世代長度為3.8年。
成年雄性白喉磯鶇的下巴和喉部有白色斑塊。 然而，其下側的其餘部分為栗橙色。 成年雌鳥的上部為棕色或橄欖棕色，下部則有明顯的鱗狀斑紋。年幼的雄鳥羽毛為灰色或金棕色，並具有橙色的下側。 鳥體部分呈現鈷藍色和黑色。 其叫聲被形容為"憂鬱、長笛般，拖長的上升哨音"。


行為
白喉磯鶇在5月至9月間棲息於其繁殖地。其繁殖地位於混合山地森林。它們在5月至7月之間繁殖，會生兩窩。鳥類的杯狀巢由樹木物質、地衣、細根和苔蘚構成內部，外部則是松針和植物莖。
白喉磯鶇的食物主要是無脊椎動物，包括象鼻蟲、蟋蟀和鱗翅目昆蟲。
白喉磯鶇是一種完全遷徙的物種。


分佈與棲地
白喉磯鶇的分佈範圍很大，約1,710,000平方公里。它原生並繁殖於滿洲、俄羅斯遠東地區及鄰近地區；在冬季遷徙至中南半島和中國南部。 偶爾可以在日本看到這種鳥。 牠們也曾在香港和新加坡被觀察到。 雖然其總體數量未知，但並無上升或下降的趨勢。
據說白喉磯鶇在北韓十分稀少。 然而，在中國相對常見，除了沿海地區。
白喉磯鶇的IUCN紅色名錄分類為"無危"。
白喉磯鶇棲息於森林、灌木叢和岩石區域。其生活的海拔範圍為0米（0英尺）至1,500米（4,900英尺）。


歷史
白喉磯鶇首次由斯文豪於1863年描述。
在1988至2000年間，白喉磯鶇在IUCN紅色名錄的分類為"低風險"和"無危"。自2000年以來，唯一的分類為"無危"。
在現代，白喉磯鶇被廣泛飼養為寵物。在某些國家，牠們也被食用。
